text.title: ES Fundamentals Procedure
text.summary: 5-step procedure to decide whether an aggregate should be event-sourced and to draft its event catalog.

step.name: assess-audit-need
step.action: List: do regulators require full history? Do customers ever ask "what did this look like at time T"? Does the team need time-travel debugging? If at least one yes, ES is a candidate.

step.name: assess-schema-stability
step.action: Count breaking entity changes in the last 6 months. > 3 = schema thrashes, defer ES until stable. ≤ 3 = ES viable; commit to versioning discipline.
step.decision-gate: If schema thrashes, choose state-stored aggregate + domain events; STOP.

step.name: draft-event-catalog
step.action: List past-tense business facts the aggregate emits. One event per meaningful state change. Reject CRUD-shaped names (`Updated`, `Changed`, `Set*`).

step.name: define-stream-strategy
step.action: Pin: stream_id = aggregate identity (UUID or composite). Each instance gets its own stream. Document in the decision record.

step.name: write-decision-record
step.action: Produce ADR: ES decision (yes/no/defer), rationale, audit need, schema stability, event catalog, stream strategy, projections planned, snapshot policy.
